dos: [DO #1 — e.g. 'Open with a fact bomb, a direct challenge to the viewer, or a specific result that earns skepticism.']
[DO #2 — e.g. 'Ground every Reel in one concrete practitioner moment — a real client situation, a specific experiment, a named system.']
[DO #3 — e.g. 'Name the audience explicitly at least once per Reel. ("If you're a B2B founder posting on LinkedIn...")']
[DO #4 — e.g. 'Write for the ear — read every spoken line aloud before finalising. If it sounds like a caption, rewrite it.']
[DO #5 — e.g. 'Use on-screen text to emphasise the sharpest phrase per beat — the mechanism name, the number, the contrarian claim.']
[DO #6 — e.g. 'Keep the script to one idea. One tension, one resolution.']
[DO #7 — e.g. 'Position yourself as the practitioner who fixed the problem, not the observer who noticed it.']
[DO #8 — e.g. 'End with a single low-friction verbal CTA — a keyword DM trigger spoken casually, not pushed.']
[DO #9 — e.g. 'Suggest B-roll that is authentic to the creator's real environment — desk, screen, hands, outdoors — not stock footage energy.']
[DO #10 — e.g. 'Grammar-check every spoken line. An error in a content-quality brand destroys the premise before the idea lands.']
donts: [DON'T #1 — e.g. 'Don't write passive observer hooks: "I ran X through Y," "Here's what I noticed" — no tension, no stakes.']
[DON'T #2 — e.g. 'Don't open with slow context-setting or generic industry observations.']
[DON'T #3 — e.g. 'Don't write spoken lines that sound like captions being read aloud — they must feel natural when spoken.']
[DON'T #4 — e.g. 'Don't try to cover more than one idea per Reel.']
[DON'T #5 — e.g. 'Don't stack multiple CTAs or create new keyword triggers if existing ones are already running — consolidate.']
[DON'T #6 — e.g. 'Don't suggest trending audio, filters, or gimmicks.']
[DON'T #7 — e.g. 'Don't suggest generic stock-video B-roll ("diverse team smiling at laptop").']
[DON'T #8 — e.g. 'Don't add motivational sign-offs, "drop a comment if you agree," or engagement-bait questions.']
[DON'T #9 — e.g. 'Don't use a vague "you" — always name the specific type of person being addressed.']
[DON'T #10 — e.g. 'Don't invent client names, statistics, or results the creator didn't mention.']
